intended_use: MuscleView 2.0 is a magnetic resonance diagnostic software device used in adults and pediatrics aged 18 and older which automatically segments muscle, bone, fat and other anatomical structures from ma
intended_use_full: MuscleView 2.0 is a magnetic resonance diagnostic software device used in adults and pediatrics aged 18 and older which automatically segments muscle, bone, fat and other anatomical structures from magnetic resonance imaging. After segmentation, it enables the generation, display and review of magnetic resonance imaging data. The segmentation results need to be reviewed and edited using appropriate software. Other physical parameters derived from the images may also be produced. This device is not intended for use with patients who have tumors in the trunk, arms and/or lower limb(s). When interpreted by a trained clinician, these images and physical parameters may yield information that may assist in diagnosis.
target_condition: Musculoskeletal assessment; not intended for patients with tumors in trunk, arms, or lower limbs
ground_truth: Manual segmentation performed by expert segmentation analysts using a consensus process to establish ground truth labels; validation compared AI results to reference standard of expert manual segmentation
other_metrics: Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) ranges from 0.755-0.997 across 172 regions of interest (ROIs); Volume Difference (VDt) reported for specific structures; performance metrics reported for healthy vs. patient subgroups, by MRI manufacturer (GE, Siemens, Philips, Canon, Toshiba), and by age/biologic sex subgroups; AI model performance stated to be 'within interobserver variability range observed among experts' and 'within predeﬁned interobserver ranges for nearly all ROIs evaluated'
